title: Hemp seed cold-process soap
subtitle: A conditioning bar with hemp seed, olive, and coconut oils at 5% superfat
excerpt: Hemp seed oil is high in linoleic and gamma-linolenic acid and produces a soft, conditioning lather. Combined with olive oil for creaminess and coconut oil for hardness and foam, this makes a well-balanced bar suited to normal and combination skin. Cure for four to six weeks.
sourceNotes: SAP values (sodium hydroxide per gram of oil) used in calculations: olive oil 0.134, coconut oil 0.190, hemp seed oil 0.135, castor oil 0.128. 5% superfat applied. Reference: The Handcrafter's Companion, Anne L. Watson. Hemp seed oil in cold-process soap: conditioning properties and shelf-life considerations documented in soap-making literature.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Always run the revised oil weights through a lye calculator before scaling -- do not simply multiply the lye weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cold-process soap requires a 48-hour rest in the mould before unmoulding, then a 4 to 6-week cure on a rack before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Cured bars keep for one year at room temperature on an open soap dish. Hemp seed oil has a shorter shelf life than olive oil; discard if bars develop a rancid smell or orange oily spots (DOS -- dreaded orange spots, a sign of rancidity).

glossaryTerms.definition: The alkali that reacts with oils in cold-process soap making (saponification). Always add lye to water, never water to lye. Wear goggles, gloves, and ensure ventilation during the lye-water mixing step.

content.text: Hemp seed oil makes a noticeably soft and conditioning bar -- it will not produce a hard, long-lasting soap on its own, which is why coconut oil anchors the recipe. The 30 g of castor oil is included to boost and bind the lather. The finished bar is suited to frequent hand-washing and gentle body use. Like all cold-process soaps, it requires a full cure before the lye is neutralised and the bar is safe to use. Always handle

Lye causes chemical burns on contact with skin and eyes. Wear safety goggles and nitrile gloves throughout. Work in a ventilated area -- the lye-water mixture releases heat and fumes when first mixed. Add the lye to the water, never the reverse. Keep children and pets out of the workspace during the lye steps.

items.prepNote: 99% purity cosmetic-grade NaOH; calculated at 5% superfat
about 2.8% of oil weight; add at light trace

content.text: Wearing goggles and gloves, weigh 175 g of distilled water into the HDPE lye pitcher. Weigh the sodium hydroxide separately. Add the lye slowly to the water, stirring as you add. The mixture will heat sharply and release fumes; do not breathe over the pitcher. Set aside to cool to 40 to 50°C.

Melt the coconut oil gently. Combine with the olive oil, hemp seed oil, and castor oil. Allow to cool to 40 to 50°C. The hemp seed oil will give the blend a slight greenish tint -- this is normal and fades significantly during cure. Aim to have both the oils and the lye solution at a similar temperature (within 10°C of each other).

Pour the lye solution slowly into the oil phase, stirring as you pour. Use a stick blender in short bursts alternating with hand stirring until the mixture reaches light trace -- a thin-custard consistency where a drizzle off the blender leaves a faint surface trail before sinking. Add the cedarwood essential oil at light trace and stir through. Hemp seed oil-heavy batches can move quickly to trace; avoid over-blending.

Pour into the silicone loaf mould. Cover and wrap in a towel to insulate (this encourages a full gel phase). Leave undisturbed for 48 hours. Unmould and slice into bars with the wire cutter. Place cut-side up on a rack in a ventilated space.

Cure for a minimum of four weeks, ideally six. During cure the remaining lye neutralises completely and the water evaporates, producing a harder, milder bar. Test with pH paper at four weeks -- finished soap reads 9 to 10. Because hemp seed oil is prone to rancidity, store finished bars on an open rack rather than in a closed container, and use within one year of curing.
